scenarios.title: Solo Django consultant seeking recurring revenue
scenarios.builder_profile: A solo Django and PostgreSQL consultant has six years of delivery experience and direct relationships with small B2B software teams.
scenarios.goal: Reach $8,000 in monthly recurring revenue within twelve months.
constraints: Can invest at most twenty hours per week
Has a $4,000 cash budget and no sales employee
advantages: Can ship secure back-office software quickly
preferences: Prefers a narrow B2B workflow over a consumer application

evidence.observation: Synthetic interviews show six of twelve former clients reconcile failed recurring invoices in spreadsheets every Monday.
evidence.relevance: The repeated workflow has a visible owner and measurable recovery value.
evidence.limitation: The sample comes only from the builder's existing network.

evidence.observation: A benchmark pricing page for a fictional incumbent starts at $1,200 per month and bundles broad revenue analytics.
evidence.relevance: A focused lower-complexity wedge could serve smaller teams.
evidence.limitation: Listed price does not prove willingness to switch.

evidence.observation: Three synthetic support logs describe payment failures caused by expired cards, bank declines, and missed customer follow-up.
evidence.relevance: The logs identify concrete failure categories and intervention points.
evidence.limitation: The logs do not quantify total recovered revenue.

scenarios.title: Design agency serving real-estate marketing teams
scenarios.builder_profile: A two-person design agency creates listing campaigns for regional real-estate brokers and has strong workflow automation skills but limited backend engineering depth.
scenarios.goal: Add a repeatable software product that can produce $5,000 monthly revenue.
constraints: Must launch a useful pilot in eight weeks
Cannot handle regulated transaction funds or replace licensed advice
advantages: Has access to forty broker marketing contacts
preferences: Wants a visual workflow with human approval before publication

evidence.observation: Synthetic workflow diaries show coordinators copy listing facts into five channel-specific templates for each property.
evidence.relevance: Repeated transformation and review work matches the agency's design assets.
evidence.limitation: Diary participants work at only four brokerages.

evidence.observation: Seven fictional coordinators report that correcting inconsistent prices or dates after publication is their most costly rework.
evidence.relevance: A validation checkpoint could prevent a specific expensive failure.
evidence.limitation: The evidence ranks pain but does not estimate a budget.

evidence.observation: A synthetic platform policy matrix shows every target channel permits drafts but uses a different required field set.
evidence.relevance: Draft generation with channel validation is technically bounded.
evidence.limitation: Platform policies could change and require maintenance.

scenarios.title: Security engineer with twelve months of runway
scenarios.builder_profile: An application-security engineer has twelve months of runway, experience with cloud controls, and trusted access to startup engineering leaders.
scenarios.goal: Validate a defensible security product before committing the full runway.
constraints: Must avoid storing customer secrets
Needs a paid design partner within ten weeks
preferences: Prefers preventative tooling with reviewer control

evidence.observation: Synthetic CTO interviews show teams repeatedly assemble customer security evidence from tickets, cloud consoles, and policy documents.
evidence.relevance: The mandatory workflow repeats and already consumes technical leadership time.
evidence.limitation: Interview recall may overstate time spent.

evidence.observation: A fictional incident review records two sales delays caused by outdated answers that no longer matched deployed controls.
evidence.relevance: Freshness checking creates a concrete product boundary beyond answer storage.
evidence.limitation: Two delays do not establish population frequency.

evidence.observation: A synthetic procurement survey says buyers require evidence links and accountable human approval for generated responses.
evidence.relevance: The required controls align with the builder's expertise and preferences.
evidence.limitation: Survey intent does not guarantee purchasing behavior.

scenarios.title: Data engineer productizing internal self-service
scenarios.builder_profile: A senior data engineer has built internal catalog and quality tools for mid-sized companies and can collaborate with one part-time product designer.
scenarios.goal: Turn a repeated internal capability into a sustainable small-team SaaS.
constraints: Cannot require a six-month data migration
Initial product must integrate read-only with common warehouses
preferences: Wants usage-based value without per-seat friction

evidence.observation: Synthetic request logs show analysts ask the same metric-definition questions across chat, tickets, and meetings.
evidence.relevance: Repeated questions can be mapped to existing metadata and ownership.
evidence.limitation: Logs are drawn from two fictional companies.

evidence.observation: Five benchmark interviews describe abandoned catalog rollouts because maintaining descriptions became a separate manual project.
evidence.relevance: Automatic workflow capture may differentiate from a static catalog.
evidence.limitation: The interviews do not isolate catalog design from change-management failures.

evidence.observation: A synthetic cost ledger attributes thirty analyst hours per month to locating owners and validating definitions.
evidence.relevance: The ledger gives a measurable value hypothesis for a narrow test.
evidence.limitation: Attributed hours are estimates rather than observed time tracking.

scenarios.title: Logistics operators building exception software
scenarios.builder_profile: A former freight dispatcher and a full-stack engineer understand regional logistics operations and have relationships with small carrier managers.
scenarios.goal: Find a high-frequency operational wedge that ten carriers will pay for.
constraints: Cannot install vehicle hardware
Must work with incomplete and delayed status data
advantages: Warm access to fifteen carrier offices
preferences: Prefers exception handling over another broad transportation suite

evidence.observation: Synthetic dispatch logs show late pickup updates trigger repeated calls among dispatchers, drivers, and customer representatives.
evidence.relevance: The exception creates costly coordination with identifiable participants.
evidence.limitation: The logs cover regional freight rather than all carrier segments.

evidence.observation: A fictional carrier manager reports that existing suite alerts are noisy because they ignore customer-specific escalation rules.
evidence.relevance: Configurable escalation could be a focused wedge around incumbent data.
evidence.limitation: One manager's complaint may not generalize.

evidence.observation: A synthetic integration inventory shows target carriers already export status events through email, CSV, or basic webhooks.
evidence.relevance: The product can start without new hardware or a full system replacement.
evidence.limitation: Data timeliness remains inconsistent across sources.

scenarios.title: Accountant with trusted small-business distribution
scenarios.builder_profile: An accountant who advises one hundred small businesses can fund a contract developer and understands recurring bookkeeping review workflows.
scenarios.goal: Launch a low-support product that improves client operations and creates recurring income.
constraints: Must not present automated tax or legal advice
Can spend only ten hours per week on the product
preferences: Wants clear human review and a simple monthly price

evidence.observation: Synthetic month-end checklists show missing receipts and unclear transaction descriptions cause repeated client follow-up.
evidence.relevance: The pattern is recurring, bounded, and precedes expert accounting review.
evidence.limitation: Checklist frequency varies by client bookkeeping quality.

evidence.observation: A fictional time study assigns four staff hours per client each month to requesting and matching missing documents.
evidence.relevance: The time estimate supports a direct operational savings hypothesis.
evidence.limitation: Staff may behave differently when observed.

evidence.observation: Synthetic client interviews prefer receiving one consolidated request with examples instead of several accounting-system notifications.
evidence.relevance: Consolidated communication could improve response without replacing professional judgment.
evidence.limitation: Stated preference does not measure completion rates.

scenarios.title: Healthcare operator avoiding protected health data
scenarios.builder_profile: A clinic operations manager and a no-code specialist know outpatient administration but deliberately want an initial product that stores no patient health information.
scenarios.goal: Validate a clinic operations product with five paid locations in six months.
constraints: The first version cannot ingest protected health information
Neither founder is a clinician or compliance lawyer
preferences: Prefers administrative coordination with explicit owner assignments

evidence.observation: Synthetic manager diaries show expiring vendor documents are tracked in shared calendars and email folders.
evidence.relevance: Document status and ownership can be managed without patient data.
evidence.limitation: Vendor categories differ across clinic types.

evidence.observation: A fictional audit preparation log lists repeated searches for current contracts, insurance certificates, and training attestations.
evidence.relevance: Evidence readiness is mandatory administrative work with a clear deadline.
evidence.limitation: The log does not establish willingness to buy separate software.

evidence.observation: Synthetic interviews report that broad compliance suites feel too complex for a five-location operator.
evidence.relevance: A narrow downmarket workflow may reduce setup and support burden.
evidence.limitation: The incumbent comparison is based on perceived rather than tested complexity.

scenarios.title: Open-source maintainer considering commercialization
scenarios.builder_profile: The maintainer of a moderately adopted developer tool has strong systems skills, an active contributor community, and no dedicated sales experience.
scenarios.goal: Create sustainable revenue without degrading the open-source project or community trust.
constraints: Core functionality must remain under its existing permissive license
Can hire at most one support engineer this year
advantages: Deep operational knowledge of the tool
preferences: Wants transparent boundaries between community and paid offerings

evidence.observation: Synthetic issue data shows teams repeatedly ask how to operate upgrades, backups, and access controls in production.
evidence.relevance: Operational burden may support hosting or governance value around the project.
evidence.limitation: Issue authors are not necessarily budget owners.

evidence.observation: A fictional user survey ranks predictable upgrades above new core features for company deployments.
evidence.relevance: The result points toward reliability rather than withholding open features.
evidence.limitation: Survey respondents self-selected from active users.

evidence.observation: Synthetic contributor notes warn that paid-only documentation would reduce trust and increase duplicate support work.
evidence.relevance: The evidence constrains monetization models that harm the community commons.
evidence.limitation: Contributor views may differ from company buyers.

scenarios.title: E-commerce agency productizing retention work
scenarios.builder_profile: A small e-commerce agency manages storefront operations for thirty specialty retailers and can build platform extensions and analytics dashboards.
scenarios.goal: Productize one recurring agency workflow into a $10,000 monthly SaaS line.
constraints: Must comply with marketplace and merchant data terms
Cannot depend on replacing the merchant's email platform
preferences: Prefers measurable revenue recovery over vanity analytics

evidence.observation: Synthetic campaign reviews show promotion exclusions are manually reconciled across catalog tags, discount rules, and email segments.
evidence.relevance: The reconciliation is repeated and errors directly affect margin or customer trust.
evidence.limitation: Existing agency clients may use unusually complex catalogs.

evidence.observation: A fictional postmortem records three margin-loss events caused by stacked discounts that violated campaign intent.
evidence.relevance: Preventing a measurable failure offers clearer value than another reporting view.
evidence.limitation: Three events do not establish expected annual loss.

evidence.observation: Synthetic operator interviews say they would test a preflight report but will not grant automatic price-change permission.
evidence.relevance: A read-only approval workflow fits buyer risk tolerance and platform constraints.
evidence.limitation: Test interest is not a paid commitment.

scenarios.title: Construction manager with mobile workflow access
scenarios.builder_profile: A former commercial construction project manager and a mobile developer have access to subcontractor supervisors and understand field documentation handoffs.
scenarios.goal: Win three paid pilots for a field-first workflow within four months.
constraints: Must function with intermittent connectivity
Cannot require every subcontractor to adopt a new account
preferences: Wants a small workflow that complements existing project systems

evidence.observation: Synthetic closeout records show supervisors chase equipment manuals and warranty photos weeks after installation.
evidence.relevance: Capturing evidence at installation could prevent a late, expensive information gap.
evidence.limitation: Closeout practices vary by general contractor.

evidence.observation: A fictional field study finds subcontractors readily send photos by text but resist full project-management accounts.
evidence.relevance: Low-friction capture is a required product and distribution constraint.
evidence.limitation: Observed crews may be more cooperative than the wider market.

evidence.observation: Synthetic manager estimates attribute ten hours per project to labeling, sorting, and requesting missing closeout evidence.
evidence.relevance: The estimate supports a bounded labor-savings test with project managers.
evidence.limitation: Self-reported hours have not been independently measured.

scenarios.title: Compliance consultant reducing repeated evidence work
scenarios.builder_profile: A privacy compliance consultant serves software vendors, understands evidence review, and can partner with a backend engineer for a focused product.
scenarios.goal: Create recurring revenue while reserving expert time for ambiguous cases.
constraints: Software cannot promise legal compliance
Every consequential output needs traceable evidence and human approval
preferences: Prefers exception review over autonomous decisions

evidence.observation: Synthetic project logs show consultants repeatedly map the same policy clauses to different customer questionnaire wording.
evidence.relevance: A traceable first-pass mapping can reduce repeated expert work.
evidence.limitation: Questionnaire formats and clause quality vary substantially.

evidence.observation: A fictional client review finds stale policy versions caused more corrections than incorrect clause interpretation.
evidence.relevance: Version freshness may be a safer and more measurable product wedge.
evidence.limitation: One review may reflect that client's document practices.

evidence.observation: Synthetic buyers require every suggested response to show its source passage and last-reviewed owner.
evidence.relevance: Provenance and reviewer control are explicit adoption requirements.
evidence.limitation: Requirements do not reveal acceptable price.

scenarios.title: Technical educator with an engaged audience
scenarios.builder_profile: A technical educator has an audience of fifteen thousand junior developers, creates practical coding courses, and can build lightweight web applications.
scenarios.goal: Develop a subscription product that improves learner outcomes beyond selling more videos.
constraints: Must avoid high-touch one-to-one tutoring
Can produce only four hours of new content per week
advantages: Owns a large library of exercises
Can reach learners directly through a newsletter
preferences: Wants visible skill progress and honest feedback rather than engagement tricks

evidence.observation: Synthetic course data shows learners complete videos but repeatedly stop before submitting portfolio projects.
evidence.relevance: The gap is between content consumption and applied proof of skill.
evidence.limitation: Completion behavior may differ outside this educator's audience.

evidence.observation: A fictional learner survey ranks choosing project scope and knowing what to fix above access to more lessons.
evidence.relevance: Structured project review could add value without a large content burden.
evidence.limitation: Survey answers may not predict subscription retention.

evidence.observation: Synthetic newsletter tests show short project teardown emails receive twice the reply rate of course discount emails.
evidence.relevance: The audience responds to applied feedback and provides a reachable test channel.
evidence.limitation: Reply rate is not equivalent to paid demand.

scenarios.title: Quality engineer serving small manufacturers
scenarios.builder_profile: A manufacturing quality engineer and an industrial software developer understand corrective-action workflows at small component suppliers.
scenarios.goal: Build a product that can support a high-value annual contract with twenty plants.
constraints: Cannot connect directly to machinery in the first release
Must preserve customer-controlled approval and audit trails
advantages: Can access six plant managers for discovery
preferences: Prefers a focused corrective-action tool over a full manufacturing suite

evidence.observation: Synthetic audit findings show corrective actions are tracked across spreadsheets, email attachments, and meeting notes.
evidence.relevance: Fragmented ownership and evidence create a bounded coordination problem.
evidence.limitation: Audit findings describe failure cases rather than everyday workflow frequency.

evidence.observation: A fictional manager study says late supplier responses cause more delay than root-cause analysis itself.
evidence.relevance: External follow-up and deadline risk may be the most valuable initial wedge.
evidence.limitation: Managers may understate internal analysis problems.

evidence.observation: Synthetic procurement notes require exportable histories and named approval owners before pilot approval.
evidence.relevance: Auditability and reviewer control are non-negotiable product requirements.
evidence.limitation: Procurement requirements may lengthen early sales.

scenarios.title: Legal operations specialist serving small teams
scenarios.builder_profile: A legal operations specialist and a workflow engineer have experience organizing commercial contract processes but will not automate legal judgment.
scenarios.goal: Create a useful operations product for companies without dedicated legal operations staff.
constraints: Must not provide legal advice or negotiate terms autonomously
Needs a low-configuration pilot deployable in one day
preferences: Wants administrative visibility and escalation rather than document generation

evidence.observation: Synthetic intake logs show contract requests arrive through chat, email, and forms with missing owners and deadlines.
evidence.relevance: Normalization and ownership can improve operations without interpreting legal terms.
evidence.limitation: The logs represent technology companies only.

evidence.observation: A fictional cycle-time review attributes most avoidable delay to unanswered business questions before legal review starts.
evidence.relevance: Pre-review completeness is a testable administrative bottleneck.
evidence.limitation: Cycle-time attribution relies on manually coded reasons.

evidence.observation: Synthetic interviews say requesters want status visibility but legal reviewers do not want another document repository.
evidence.relevance: A lightweight coordination layer could complement existing storage.
evidence.limitation: Integration expectations remain unspecified.

scenarios.title: Nonprofit grants operator with sector access
scenarios.builder_profile: A former nonprofit grants manager and a generalist developer understand reporting obligations and can interview small nonprofit directors.
scenarios.goal: Build an affordable product with repeatable distribution through nonprofit networks.
constraints: Target organizations have limited software budgets
The product cannot depend on replacing accounting systems
advantages: Has access to two nonprofit associations
preferences: Wants transparent time savings and accessible pricing

evidence.observation: Synthetic reporting calendars show program evidence is requested from the same internal owners shortly before each grant deadline.
evidence.relevance: Earlier recurring collection could reduce deadline risk and staff interruption.
evidence.limitation: Grant requirements differ across funders.

evidence.observation: A fictional director survey identifies missed narrative evidence, not financial totals, as the most common report scramble.
evidence.relevance: The wedge can complement accounting rather than replace it.
evidence.limitation: Directors may not observe every staff bottleneck.

evidence.observation: Synthetic association notes show member workshops reach many organizations but vendors rarely offer shared onboarding.
evidence.relevance: Association-led cohort onboarding may reduce acquisition and support cost.
evidence.limitation: Workshop attendance does not imply purchasing authority.

scenarios.title: Restaurant group operator reducing location variance
scenarios.builder_profile: An operations director from a twelve-location restaurant group and a mobile web developer understand manager checklists and vendor coordination.
scenarios.goal: Validate a product with multi-location operators while keeping frontline usage simple.
constraints: Frontline tasks must take under two minutes
Cannot require new point-of-sale hardware or integrations initially
preferences: Prefers exception visibility over exhaustive task tracking

evidence.observation: Synthetic district reviews show recurring equipment issues are logged inconsistently and rediscovered during weekly calls.
evidence.relevance: Lightweight capture and escalation may prevent repeated operational loss.
evidence.limitation: Equipment failure patterns vary by restaurant format.

evidence.observation: A fictional manager time study finds long daily checklists are often completed retrospectively without useful notes.
evidence.relevance: Exception-first interaction better fits frontline time constraints.
evidence.limitation: Observed behavior may change during the study.

evidence.observation: Synthetic operator interviews value proof of resolution across locations more than another aggregate score.
evidence.relevance: Resolution evidence supplies a specific buyer-facing outcome.
evidence.limitation: The interviews do not quantify contract value.

scenarios.title: Climate analyst focused on procurement evidence
scenarios.builder_profile: A climate data analyst and a procurement software engineer know supplier-data workflows and want to avoid unsupported sustainability claims.
scenarios.goal: Create a credible supplier operations product for mid-market manufacturers.
constraints: Must display source limitations and never invent missing measurements
Cannot require suppliers to buy software
advantages: Can normalize messy public and supplier data
Has access to eight procurement leaders
preferences: Wants decision support with provenance rather than a headline score

evidence.observation: Synthetic supplier reviews show buyers repeatedly request the same facility and material evidence in different spreadsheet formats.
evidence.relevance: Reusable evidence mapping can reduce mandatory coordination work.
evidence.limitation: The benchmark does not establish accuracy of supplier submissions.

evidence.observation: A fictional procurement log records delays when analysts cannot distinguish missing data from an explicit zero value.
evidence.relevance: Data-state clarity is a narrow, testable workflow improvement.
evidence.limitation: Delay costs have not been monetized.

evidence.observation: Synthetic buyer interviews reject opaque composite scores but accept field-level provenance and confidence labels.
evidence.relevance: The preferred design aligns with the founders' evidence skills.
evidence.limitation: Acceptance in interviews does not prove routine usage.

scenarios.title: Niche recruiter with a trusted candidate network
scenarios.builder_profile: An independent recruiter specializes in industrial automation roles, maintains a trusted candidate network, and can hire a contract developer.
scenarios.goal: Reduce placement volatility by adding recurring employer revenue.
constraints: Must not make autonomous hiring decisions
Can support no more than five pilot employers personally
advantages: Has direct access to hiring managers and candidates
preferences: Wants structured workflow support rather than a generic job board

evidence.observation: Synthetic intake reviews show employers reuse old job descriptions that omit the actual equipment and shift constraints.
evidence.relevance: Improving intake specificity can reduce wasted sourcing before any hiring judgment.
evidence.limitation: The evidence comes from the recruiter's own placements.

evidence.observation: A fictional rejection analysis finds late discovery of travel requirements causes more candidate withdrawals than compensation mismatch.
evidence.relevance: Early constraint validation is a measurable operational wedge.
evidence.limitation: Coded withdrawal reasons may be incomplete.

evidence.observation: Synthetic hiring managers say they will pay for shorter intake cycles but require final candidate review to remain human.
evidence.relevance: The buyer outcome and control boundary are both explicit.
evidence.limitation: Stated payment intent lacks a signed pilot.

scenarios.title: Risk analyst productizing policy operations
scenarios.builder_profile: A former fintech risk analyst and a backend engineer understand internal policy attestations but are not building regulated financial advice or automated approvals.
scenarios.goal: Find a low-liability operational product with enterprise expansion potential.
constraints: Must not approve customers or transactions
Needs immutable history and customer-controlled retention
advantages: Can reach ten compliance operations managers
preferences: Prefers accountable reviewer queues over automated decisions

evidence.observation: Synthetic audit preparation shows control owners repeatedly re-answer who approved a policy exception and which evidence was current.
evidence.relevance: Provenance and freshness are mandatory operational questions.
evidence.limitation: Audit preparation is periodic rather than daily.

evidence.observation: A fictional exception register contains duplicate entries because teams use separate ticket and spreadsheet identifiers.
evidence.relevance: Cross-system identity and status reconciliation is a concrete workflow problem.
evidence.limitation: The example may reflect poor local process rather than market need.

evidence.observation: Synthetic manager interviews say broad governance suites take months to configure for a single exception workflow.
evidence.relevance: A narrower deployable layer could move an enterprise capability downmarket.
evidence.limitation: Configuration estimates have not been independently verified.

scenarios.title: Property manager coordinating recurring inspections
scenarios.builder_profile: A residential property manager with a portfolio of eight hundred units and a full-stack developer understand vendor, tenant, and owner communication workflows.
scenarios.goal: Pilot a recurring operations product that can expand to similar regional managers.
constraints: Cannot replace the core property-management system
Initial workflow must avoid collecting rent or making tenancy decisions
advantages: Can pilot against real operational schedules
Has relationships with regional property managers
preferences: Wants evidence of completion and clear exception ownership

evidence.observation: Synthetic inspection logs show smoke-alarm and seasonal maintenance evidence arrives through vendor texts, email, and paper invoices.
evidence.relevance: Fragmented proof creates recurring reconciliation and owner-reporting work.
evidence.limitation: Inspection obligations vary by jurisdiction and property type.

evidence.observation: A fictional operations review finds staff spend more time chasing missing photos and unit identifiers than scheduling vendors.
evidence.relevance: Capture completeness is a narrower wedge than full vendor management.
evidence.limitation: The time split is based on one portfolio.

evidence.observation: Synthetic peer interviews require exports into existing systems and reject a second resident communication portal.
evidence.relevance: The product must complement the incumbent and focus on internal evidence flow.
evidence.limitation: Export requirements differ by incumbent platform.
